name: Resource One Credit Union
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving Dallas County and surrounding Texas cities with comprehensive personal and business banking solutions. Federally insured by NCUA.
description_long: Resource One Credit Union is a federally insured, member-owned credit union operating as a not-for-profit financial institution. The organization serves individuals and businesses in a defined geographic area across North Texas, including Mesquite, Garland, Irving, Cedar Hill, and Dallas County. Established as a community-focused alternative to traditional banks, Resource One operates under the credit union model where members are part-owners of the institution. The credit union offers a broad range of personal and business banking products. Personal services include spending and savings accounts, credit cards, mortgages, auto loans, auto refinances, personal loans, and what they term "adventure loans." Business banking solutions are also available to qualifying members. Members can access their accounts through multiple channels: physical branch locations, mobile banking applications available on iOS and Android platforms, and phone banking via their customer service line (1-800-375-3674). Resource One distinguishes itself through its member-ownership model and community focus within its defined field of membership. The institution emphasizes accessibility, committing publicly to WCAG 2.0 website accessibility guidelines and physical branch accessibility for members with disabilities. The credit union maintains an active digital presence with a mobile app, podcast (Financial Coffee™), and newsletter (The Source) aimed at financial education. While the main website footer indicates the routing number (311080162) and general service offerings, detailed information about individual branches, specific loan terms, interest rates, and account features could not be verified from the provided website content. Prospective members should contact the credit union directly to confirm current services and eligibility.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Federally insured by NCUA, providing deposit protection up to standard limits
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means profits theoretically benefit members rather than external shareholders
Membership available to anyone living or working in Mesquite, Garland, Irving, Cedar Hill, or Dallas County—relatively accessible field of membership
Multiple account types including spending accounts, savings, credit cards, mortgages, and auto loans—comprehensive financial services
Mobile banking apps available on both iOS (Apple Store) and Android (Google Play) for convenient account access
Committed to accessibility standards including WCAG 2.0 compliance and physical branch accessibility for disabled members
Educational resources provided through Financial Coffee™ Podcast and The Source newsletter
cons: Limited geographic membership field—service area restricted to specific Dallas County cities and surrounding areas; not available nationwide
Branch location pages returning 404 errors prevent verification of current branch hours, locations, and services
No specific interest rates, APRs, fees, or terms visible on accessible website pages
No information provided about minimum deposit requirements, account minimums, or loan qualification criteria
Limited details on business banking products despite claiming to offer them
best_for: Dallas County residents and workers seeking full-service banking from a member-owned institution
Consumers who prefer credit union membership models over traditional bank structures
Small business owners in the service area needing both personal and business banking solutions
Members seeking mobile-first banking with apps on both major platforms
diagnosis: Resource One Credit Union is best for Dallas County area residents and workers seeking comprehensive banking services through a member-owned, federally insured institution. The primary caveat is the limited geographic service area (restricted to Dallas County and nearby Texas cities) and the inability to verify current specific terms, rates, or branch details due to website access limitations.
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
meta_description: Member-owned credit union serving Dallas County and surrounding Texas cities with personal and business banking solutions.
